name: Email Teaser Click-Driver — Short Click-to-Page Broadcast Email

sections: Copywriting Secrets Secret #18 — How To Write Killer Email Teasers—FAST
Copywriting Secrets Secret #6 — The #1 Single Most Important Piece of Sales Copy (subject line = headline)
Copywriting Secrets Secret #9 — The Ultimate Bullet Formula (arouse curiosity with bullets)
Copywriting Secrets Secret #13 — It's All About Them—Never About You (write to one person)

trigger.primary: Any broadcast send to the engaged list where the goal is to drive a CLICK to a page — sales letter, video, blog post, review, webinar registration, or content — and let the PAGE sell, not the email.
trigger.secondary: Affiliate or JV send — same template works when enticing a partner to email their list on your behalf.
trigger.trigger_type_ghl: Email Campaign (Broadcast) to seinfeld-broadcast Smart List, OR a workflow Send-Email step pointing to an offer/content page.
trigger.important_distinction: BOOK: 'Ninety-nine times out of a hundred, the sole purpose of that email teaser is to get the reader to click a link in the email itself and go to a web page.' This is NOT a story email; it is a SHORT CLICK-DRIVER. The page (sales letter, VSL, blog) does the selling — the email's only job is the click.

sequence.purpose: Earn the open — same function as a headline; if it fails, nothing else matters.
sequence.subjectAngle: Short, concise, often a question. Book examples: 'Worried about failing your next PT test?' / 'Failed your last PT test?' / 'PT test coming up?' / 'This blew me away' / 'Want to be a published author?' — subject lines about THEM, not you. Questions that prequalify the right opener and uninterest the wrong one.

sequence.purpose: Personal acknowledgment; make them feel seen, not like list member #482,000.
sequence.bodyFramework: Use first name merge if possible: 'Hey {{contact.first_name}},' — book: 'Make people feel like they're part of the group and being acknowledged.' If no merge tag: 'Hey everyone,' or 'Hi fellow [tribe identity]' (e.g., 'Hi Fellow Funnel Hacker,'). Never skip it.

sequence.purpose: Pop the reader out of hypnosis; get them leaning in.
sequence.bodyFramework: One punchy statement OR an open question. Book options: 'Got a great new video for you' / 'Got something that's going to blow your mind' / 'Did you know that 99 out of 100 people who try to write a book fail?' / 'This was totally unexpected.' Short. One sentence.

sequence.purpose: Build enough curiosity that clicking feels like the obvious next move.
sequence.bodyFramework: Two, three, or four short bullets (NOT long benefit bullets). Each bullet arouses curiosity about what is on the other side of the link. Book: 'Once you have their attention, hit them with three or four bullets to arouse curiosity.' Ultimate Bullet Formula applied: 'It ___ so you can ___ which means ___.' BUT kept SHORT — this is a teaser, not a sales page. 'Pumping up the benefits of what they will see, not of what you're selling.'

sequence.purpose: Give a specific action to take right now.
sequence.bodyFramework: One simple directional instruction + the link. Book: 'Specific call to action for what you want them to do.' Example: 'Click here right now to see it.' / 'Go check it out.' / 'You've got to check this out.' Do NOT pre-sell the offer. The LINK destination sells.

sequence.purpose: Reinforce the friend-to-friend tone; keep it human.
sequence.bodyFramework: Book: 'Close with a personal note like Hey, I'll see you over there. Thanks, Jim.' — 'I'll see you over there' signals they're going to a place they'd WANT to be. Keep it one line. Add your name — no formal email footer needed for warm lists.

formatRules.length: SHORT. Book: 'If you must err on the side of one or the other, go with a shorter email as opposed to a longer one.' 40-60% of readers see this on mobile — long text kills response.
formatRules.tone: Book: 'Email is a personal way to communicate because it arrives in their inbox. Therefore, they feel like they know you. So the more you talk to them in the quick, familiar language of a friend, the better your results.' + 'Remember: Friends don't send friends ten-page sales letters in email!'
formatRules.purpose_boundary: Book (non-negotiable): 'Most people make the mistake of selling the offer in the email teaser. Don't! Your sales letter or video sales letter will sell them.' The teaser prepares; the page converts.
formatRules.oneVsMany: Book: 'Though you may send that email to a million people, remember this: they will read it one person at a time.' Write to ONE person — your George (your ideal reader buddy).

short_teaser.subject: This blew me away
short_teaser.body:
Hey [first name],

This was totally unexpected.

Somebody just did a review and it's amazing!

You've got to check this out.

[LINK]

See you over there,
[Name]
short_teaser.source: Direct from Copywriting Secrets Secret #18 — 'One of the most successful email teasers I ever sent to my own list of prospects was only a few lines.'

bullet_teaser.subject: Want to be a published author?
bullet_teaser.body:
Hey [first name],

Did you know that 99 out of 100 people who try to write a book fail?

Yep. It sucks. The main reasons why they fail are:
- They don't know how to create content that sells.
- They don't know how to get it formatted.
- They don't know how to publish it.

The cool news is I have a great new video that shows you exactly how to solve all those problems—fast and easy!

Click here right now to see it.

[LINK]

I'll see you over there.

Thanks,
[Name]
bullet_teaser.source: Direct from Copywriting Secrets Secret #18 example.

differentFromSeinfeldEmail.seinfeld_email: 90% entertainment story → Stealth/Columbo pivot → tie to offer in the email. 200-500+ words. Sells the relationship daily.
differentFromSeinfeldEmail.email_teaser: Short (40-100 words). No story arc. Sole job = get the CLICK to a page. The page sells. Used when you have a specific content piece, video, sales letter, review, or webinar to drive traffic to.
differentFromSeinfeldEmail.when_to_use_which: Seinfeld email: daily engagement/bonding sends. Email teaser: promotion sends, content drives, affiliate JV sends, or any time you need pure click-through traffic to a specific page.

copyPersona.primary: Jim Edwards (Copywriting Secrets) — short, punchy, friend-to-friend, curiosity-first, ONE click action. Secret #18 is the definitive source.
copyPersona.secondary: Edwards secondary supports: subject line = headline (Secret #6), Ultimate Bullet Formula curiosity-first version (Secret #9), write to one person not a crowd (Secret #13).

ghlBuild.buildType: Email Campaign (Broadcast) OR a Send-Email action step inside a workflow — NOT a standalone autoresponder.
ghlBuild.template: Create as 'Email Teaser Shell' in Marketing > Emails > Templates. Minimal design: plain text feel, first-name merge, no heavy image headers, one hyperlinked CTA text or button, no P.S. (length discipline).
ghlBuild.audience: Same seinfeld-broadcast Smart List (or any warm-list segment) as the daily broadcast; can also be sent as a standalone one-off Campaign.
ghlBuild.affiliateSend: When used for JV/affiliate purposes: provide the completed teaser copy as a ready-to-send .txt or HTML snippet so the partner can paste and deploy with zero editing. Keep it short enough that the partner actually sends it.
ghlBuild.notes: Do not confuse this with the Seinfeld Broadcast Shell — they are two different GHL email templates for two different jobs. Set action order + parentKey before first save in any workflow context (Skill 44 reliability). Track click-through rate as the primary metric (not open rate).

flexibility.mode1_explicit_desire: User has specific copy they want in the email — use this template only as optional reference for format/structure. Never override their words.
flexibility.mode2_unsure: Suggest the email teaser as the go-to for traffic-driving sends; explain the SHORT is the feature, not the bug. Let user decide.
flexibility.mode3_just_do_it: Build from the template: fill subject, salutation, 1-2 sentence interrupt, 2-3 bullets, CTA link, personal close. Done in 5 minutes.
flexibility.always_overridable: Subject line angle, bullet count, length, and closing phrase are all preferences — the user can adjust any element without breaking the concept.

kpis: unsubscribe rate (signals mismatch between teaser promise and page reality)
faithfulness: The 6-part anatomy (subject / salutation / shocking statement / 2-4 bullets / CTA / personal close), both worked examples, the 'friends don't send friends ten-page sales letters' rule, the 99-out-of-100-purpose, and the shorter-is-better discipline are taken directly from Copywriting Secrets Secret #18. The distinction from the Seinfeld story email is derived from combining DotCom Secrets Secret #8 and Copywriting Secrets Secret #18 — flagged as derived, not a single verbatim passage.
